samples.xml_description:
Implement a 4-bit unsigned number pipeline multiplier. Clock. Active-low reset. Multiplicand. Multiplier. Product. 4 The input signals (mul_a and mul_b) are extended by adding "size" number of zero bits at the most significant bit positions.
For example, if size = 4 and mul_a = 2, then mul_a_ext = 00000010.
This is done to prepare the input signals for the multiplication operation.
The multiplication operation is done using a generate block.
For each bit position i from 0 to size-1, the partial product is calculated as follows:
If the corresponding bit in the multiplier is 1, the multiplicand is left-shifted by i positions.
If the corresponding bit in the multiplier is 0, the partial product is set to 0 ('d0).
This is done using the following code:
for(i = 0; i < size; i = i + 1)
  partial_product[i] = (mul_b[i] == 1'b1) ? mul_a_ext << i : 'd0;
This is the code for the multiplication operation.
Note that the partial_product is a 2D array.
The add of partial products is done using the following code:
for(i = 0; i < size; i = i + 1)
  if(i == 0)
    sum_reg[i] = $signed(partial_product[i]);
  else
    sum_reg[i] = $signed(sum_reg[i-1]) + $signed(partial_product[i]);
This is the code for the add of partial products.
Note that sum_reg is a 2D array as well.
The final product is calculated as follows:
product = (rst_n == 0) ? 0 : sum_reg[size-1];
This is the code for the final product.
Note that this is a combinational logic.
